mechanism_narrative: Growth in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and S&P 500 earnings, leading to positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If monthly RSAFS growth is negatively correlated with 20-day SPX returns (Spearman correlation ≤ 0) over any 3-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the civilian unemployment rate (UNRATE) drops below 4.0%, the labor market becomes historically tight, raising wage inflation fears and prompting the Fed to tighten policy, which depresses equity valuations over the subsequent 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Low unemployment historically precedes short-term market corrections
failure_condition: No negative average 5-day return following UNRATE < 4.0 triggers, or positive average return significantly different from zero (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while industrial production declines, it creates a stagflationary environment that hurts corporate margins and prospects, leading to negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: No negative average 60-day return when trigger conditions are met, or positive average return significant at 5% level over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles (when FEDFUNDS is declining over a 3-month window), rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand that supports equity valuations, leading to positive forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: During easing cycles, the correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 20-day returns is not significantly positive (Spearman rank correlation < 0.1) or regime classification fails to produce distinct effect over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect expanding manufacturing output, which correlates with rising corporate earnings and higher stock prices over the next month.
failure_condition: If monthly INDPRO growth is not significantly positively correlated with 20-day forward returns (Spearman rank correlation <= 0) over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.2 million units (annualized rate), it signals a contraction in the housing market that drags down economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: No negative average 60-day return following HOUST < 1200 triggers, or false positive rate > 50% over any 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth remains strong (above 200K per month) while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is elevated above 4%, it indicates an overheating labor market that will require further tightening, leading to negative forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: No negative average 20-day return when trigger conditions are met, or positive return in more than 40% of triggered cases over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong consumer spending growth (RSAFS > 0.5% per month), rising unemployment (UNRATE) is particularly damaging to equity markets as it contrasts with robust consumption and signals fragility, leading to negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: In high-consumption regime, the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward 20-day returns is not significantly negative (Spearman rank correlation > -0.1) over rolling 3-year windows.
